description: This problem comes from a taisha joseki variation. Start with A and then find the best moves.
sgf:
(;FF[4]GM[1]CA[UTF-8]AP[goproblems:0.1.0]SZ[19]ST[0]AB[bm][cn][dn][do][dq][ep][eq][fq][hq]AW[bn][co][cp][cq][dl][dp][eo][fo][fp]LB[cm:A](;W[cm];B[bo](;W[an];B[am](;W[en];B[ao](;W[bn];B[an];W[dm]LB[cm:1]LB[bo:2]LB[bn:3]LB[em:4]C[Great job, you found the tombstone squeeze tesuji.
Of course, Black shouldn't play along with this sequence, after the throw in at 3 they should escape by playing at 4.RIGHT])(;W[dm];B[bn]C[Oops, black is fine. You were close!]))(;W[dm];B[en];W[fn]C[Requires a ladder, you can do better. ])(;W[bp];B[ao](;W[bn];B[em]C[Not quite.])(;W[en];B[bn])))(;W[en];B[an];W[dm];B[bn]TR[cq]TR[cp]TR[co]TR[dp]C[A good idea, but unfortunately the marked stones are now in a lot of trouble. Look for an even stronger squeeze.]))(;W[bo]C[Wow, you know your taisha. This is another good variation according to AI, but this problem is about knowing a specific tesuji, not knowing variations. RIGHT]))
